extract.name: Conversion System: Site, CRM, Attribution and Reporting
extract.tagline: Site, CRM, attribution and reporting in one loop.
extract.summary: A done-for-you revenue system that rebuilds the website for lead capture and wires it into a CRM with attribution and reporting in a single loop. It covers the full path from capturing a lead to qualifying, routing, closing, and measuring, with automated follow-up, nurture sequences, tracking, and weekly reporting. Priced as a one-time build plus a monthly retainer, going live in roughly 14 to 21 business days.
extract.pricing: One-time: $4,000. Recurring: $400 per month after. Timeline: fourteen to twenty-one business days.
included: CRM with owners, stages, follow-up tasks
Attribution by channel (organic, ads, referral, direct)
Tracking configuration (GA4, Meta Pixel, conversion events)
keyPoints: How it works runs as a single loop in five stages: Capture, Qualify, Route, Close, Measure
Leads are routed automatically in under two minutes
Instant alerts fire to email and Slack when a lead comes in
A four-step email and SMS nurture sequence follows up with leads
Booking and calendar sync lets leads schedule directly
Tracking is configured across GA4, Meta Pixel, and conversion events
Weekly Monday reports deliver the metrics that matter
The site rebuild is the longest step; the CRM and tracking layer is wired up in the same window
proof: 92% of leads followed up inside two minutes
faq: How long does setup take? Most builds go live in fourteen to twenty-one business days. The site rebuild is the longest step, the CRM and tracking layer is wired up in the same window.
Do I need a CRM already? (No answer text provided on page)
What does the $400 per month cover? (No answer text provided on page)
Can the system include the AI Chat Agent or Voice Agent? (No answer text provided on page)
What if I already run Google or Meta Ads? (No answer text provided on page)
extract.capgRemoved: Removed the grant/funding wrapper found on the page. Stripped the sentence: "The Canada-Alberta Job Grant covers up to 66 percent of AI training, with up to $10,000 per trainee per year." Stripped the site/category framing: "AI workforce training and digital services for Alberta operators. CAJG-eligible programs and a digital stack tuned for revenue." Stripped the FAQ question "Does this qualify for the Canada-Alberta Job Grant?" (which had no answer text on the page). All references to CAJG, Canada-Alberta Job Grant, "66 percent / 66% covered," "$10,000 per trainee per year," "Alberta operators," and "CAJG-eligible programs" were excluded from the extraction. The underlying service and its real pricing ($4,000 one-time + $400/month) were kept.

copy.seoDescription: We automate the manual work in your business. Connect your tools, sync your CRM, and run follow-ups on their own.

sections.headline: Kill the manual work
sections.subhead: We connect your tools so the busywork runs on its own.
sections.body: Copy-paste between apps. Manual follow-ups. End-of-week reports. We automate all of it, so your team stops doing what software should.
bullets: Connect the tools you already use
Follow-ups and data entry on autopilot
Less manual work, fewer dropped balls

sections.headline: Your team is doing robot work
sections.subhead: The same tasks, every day, by hand.
sections.body: Most of the day goes to moving data around and chasing things that should chase themselves. It adds up fast.
bullets: Copy-pasting the same info between tools
Leads that go cold while nobody follows up
Reports built by hand every single week
Mistakes from typing the same thing twice

sections.headline: We wire your tools together
sections.subhead: One system that talks to itself.
sections.body: We build the workflows that move your data and trigger your tasks, so nothing falls through the cracks.
bullets: n8n workflows built around how you actually work
App integrations that connect your whole stack
CRM sync so every record stays current
Automatic follow-ups by email and WhatsApp
Reports that build and send themselves

sections.headline: What this looks like in practice
bullets: Lead capture to CRM: a new form or DM creates the contact, tags it, and starts the follow-up. No typing.
Booking to reminders: a booked call lands on the calendar and fires reminders, so no-shows drop.
Weekly report on autopilot: numbers pull from your tools and land in your inbox every Monday.

sections.headline: Hours back, every week
sections.subhead: The manual work disappears, the time comes back.
sections.body: Our automations save teams real hours they used to spend on copy-paste and follow-up.
bullets: [PLACEHOLDER: X hours saved per week per client]
[PLACEHOLDER: X automations live in production]
[PLACEHOLDER: short result quote from a client]
sections.flags: Contains placeholders: hours saved, automations live, client quote. Confirm real numbers before publishing.

sections.headline: Questions before you start
bullets: Will this work with my tools? Most likely yes. We connect the apps you already use, no need to switch platforms.
How long does it take? Simple automations go live in days, larger systems in 2-3 weeks. [REVIEW: confirm timeline]
What if something breaks? We stay after launch. We monitor, fix, and adjust as your business changes.
sections.flags: [REVIEW: confirm delivery timeline in FAQ answer]

sections.headline: Let's automate your busywork
sections.subhead: Book a call and we map what to automate first.
sections.body: Tell us where the manual work hurts most. We show you what we can take off your plate.
